name: Sharpe and Sortino ratios on a return series
task: Create /tmp/eval_sharpe/ratios.py with two functions: sharpe(returns, risk_free=0.0) and sortino(returns, risk_free=0.0). Both return ANNUALIZED ratios assuming returns are daily (252 trading days/year). Sharpe = sqrt(252) * (mean(returns) - risk_free) / sample_stdev(returns). Sortino = sqrt(252) * (mean(returns) - risk_free) / downside_deviation, where downside_deviation = sqrt(mean of squared NEGATIVE deviations from risk_free, computed over ALL returns including zeros for the non-negative ones). If there are no returns below risk_free, sortino returns float('inf') (the strategy has no downside). Both raise ValueError on empty input. Pure Python — no numpy.
